SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.expander

Class ExpanderChartKhader

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.equipment.expander.ExpanderChartKhader
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class ExpanderChartKhader extends Object implements Serializable
ExpanderChartKhader is a composition-aware, two-dimensional radial-inflow expander performance map modelled on the Khader (2015) dimensionless-correction approach already used for centrifugal compressors in CompressorChartKhader2015.

Instead of representing expander performance with a single one-dimensional efficiency parabola in the velocity ratio U/C, this class stores a full grid of map data:

  • One curve for each inlet-guide-vane (IGV) position.
  • Each curve gives the isentropic efficiency and the isentropic stage head drop as a function of the velocity ratio U/C (or equivalently the corrected specific speed).

Composition awareness. The map is digitised once on a reference fluid. The stage head drop is normalised by the square of the reference fluid sound speed to give a dimensionless head coefficient. When the map is evaluated for an arbitrary process fluid the head coefficient is scaled back by the actual fluid sound speed squared:

$$ \\psi = \\frac{\\Delta h_s}{c_s^2}, \\qquad \\Delta h_s = \\psi \\, c_{s,\\text{actual}}^2 $$

Because the velocity ratio U/C is already dimensionless and Mach-number similar, the efficiency map is reused directly for any fluid. This lets the expander side carry the same fidelity as the Khader compressor map and removes the low-load solver instability caused by the single global parabola.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim

  • Constructor Details

    • ExpanderChartKhader

      public ExpanderChartKhader()
      Default constructor.

    • ExpanderChartKhader

      public ExpanderChartKhader(SystemInterface referenceFluid, double impellerDiameter)
      Constructs an ExpanderChartKhader with a reference fluid and impeller diameter.
      Parameters:
      referenceFluid - the fluid the OEM map was digitised on (may be null to skip composition correction and use the head directly)
      impellerDiameter - the impeller outer diameter in m

  • Method Details

    • setCurves

      public void setCurves(double[] igvPositions, double[][] uc, double[][] eta, double[][] headDropKjPerKg)
      Set the digitised expander performance map.

      The map is supplied as one curve per IGV position. Each curve is an array of velocity ratios with matching isentropic efficiency and isentropic stage head drop (kJ/kg). All inner arrays for a given IGV position must share the same length, but different IGV positions may use a different number of points.

      Parameters:
      igvPositions - array of IGV positions (fraction of maximum area, 0..1), strictly increasing
      uc - 2-D array of velocity ratios U/C, one row per IGV position
      eta - 2-D array of isentropic efficiencies (0..1), one row per IGV position
      headDropKjPerKg - 2-D array of isentropic stage head drops in kJ/kg, one row per IGV position
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array shapes are inconsistent

    • computeReferenceSoundSpeed

      private double computeReferenceSoundSpeed()
      Compute (and cache) the reference fluid sound speed used to normalise the head coefficient.
      Returns:
      the reference sound speed in m/s (1.0 if no reference fluid is set, leaving the head effectively un-normalised)

    • isMapDefined

      public boolean isMapDefined()
      Returns whether a performance map has been loaded.
      Returns:
      true if setCurves(double[], double[][], double[][], double[][]) has been called with valid data

    • getEfficiency

      public double getEfficiency(double uc, double igv)
      Get the isentropic efficiency at a given velocity ratio and IGV position using bilinear interpolation over the map.
      Parameters:
      uc - velocity ratio U/C
      igv - IGV position (fraction of maximum area, 0..1)
      Returns:
      the interpolated isentropic efficiency (0..1)

    • getStageHeadDrop

      public double getStageHeadDrop(double uc, double igv, SystemInterface processFluid)
      Get the isentropic stage head drop at a given velocity ratio and IGV position for the supplied process fluid. The dimensionless head coefficient is scaled by the actual fluid sound speed squared to make the result composition aware.
      Parameters:
      uc - velocity ratio U/C
      igv - IGV position (fraction of maximum area, 0..1)
      processFluid - the actual process fluid (may be null to use the reference sound speed)
      Returns:
      the isentropic stage head drop in kJ/kg

    • getOptimumVelocityRatio

      public double getOptimumVelocityRatio(double igv)
      Find the velocity ratio that maximises efficiency at a given IGV position. Useful for control targets and best-efficiency-point tracking.
      Parameters:
      igv - IGV position (fraction of maximum area, 0..1)
      Returns:
      the velocity ratio U/C at peak efficiency for the nearest stored IGV curve

    • interpolate

      private double interpolate(double uc, double igv, double[][] grid)
      Bilinear interpolation of a stored grid quantity over velocity ratio and IGV position. Linear blending is used between the two bracketing IGV curves and clamped at the edges.
      Parameters:
      uc - velocity ratio U/C
      igv - IGV position (fraction of maximum area)
      grid - the per-IGV value grid to interpolate (efficiency or head coefficient)
      Returns:
      the interpolated value

    • interpolateCurve

      private double interpolateCurve(double uc, double[] x, double[] y)
      Piecewise-linear interpolation of a single curve in velocity ratio, with linear extrapolation outside the data range.
      Parameters:
      uc - velocity ratio to evaluate
      x - sorted velocity-ratio abscissa array
      y - matching ordinate array
      Returns:
      the interpolated value

    • nearestIgvIndex

      private int nearestIgvIndex(double igv)
      Find the index of the stored IGV curve closest to the requested IGV position.
      Parameters:
      igv - IGV position (fraction of maximum area)
      Returns:
      the index of the nearest stored IGV curve

    • getReferenceSoundSpeed

      public double getReferenceSoundSpeed()
      Get the reference fluid sound speed used for head normalisation.
      Returns:
      the reference sound speed in m/s

    • getImpellerOuterDiameter

      public double getImpellerOuterDiameter()
      Get the impeller outer diameter.
      Returns:
      the impeller outer diameter in m

    • setImpellerOuterDiameter

      public void setImpellerOuterDiameter(double impellerOuterDiameter)
      Set the impeller outer diameter.
      Parameters:
      impellerOuterDiameter - the impeller outer diameter in m

    • setReferenceFluid

      public void setReferenceFluid(SystemInterface referenceFluid)
      Set the reference fluid used to normalise the digitised map.
      Parameters:
      referenceFluid - the reference fluid

    • getIgvPositions

      public double[] getIgvPositions()
      Get the IGV positions of the stored curves.
      Returns:
      a defensive copy of the IGV position array, or null if no map is defined
